evidence.claim: PAN ATDPD4055C exactly matches an active NSE/SEBI debarment order.
evidence.excerpt: Order Particulars: SEBI Interim Order in the matter of Stock Recommendations using YouTube in the scrip of Sadhna Broadcast Limited

evidence.claim: Adverse media reports the subject was restrained by SEBI in a front-running probe.
evidence.excerpt: The regulator has restrained the subject from accessing the securities market.

evidence.claim: Directorship history could not be retrieved; MCA registry lookup returned no record.

explanation: Subject's PAN exactly matches an active NSE/SEBI debarment [EV-001]. Independent adverse media corroborates the regulatory action [EV-002]. Directorship history could not be verified [EV-003] and is excluded from the risk calculation.

evidence.claim: Customer name is identical to a debarred entity, but the PANs differ.
evidence.excerpt: Customer PAN AFOPG8341M vs watchlist PAN ATDPD4055C.

explanation: No alert raised. Although the customer's name is identical to a debarred entity, the PANs differ [EV-010], which establishes them as distinct persons. Suppressed with reason logged.

evidence.claim: Name is a listed transliteration of Hafiz Muhammad Saeed, designated under the Fourth Schedule of the UAPA, 1967.
evidence.excerpt: Aliases on record: Muhammad Saeed; Hafiz Muhammad; Hafez Mohammad Saeed; Hafiz Mohaddad Sayid

evidence.claim: Adverse media co-mentions the subject with the designating authority.
evidence.excerpt: NIA names the subject in a cross-border financing chargesheet.

evidence.claim: No PAN on file for this customer; the UAPA list carries no identifiers either. No identifier-level confirmation is possible.

explanation: CRITICAL. The customer name is a listed transliteration of a UAPA-designated individual [EV-020], corroborated by adverse media naming the designating authority [EV-021]. No identifier-level confirmation is possible on either side [EV-022]; this case therefore requires human adjudication and cannot be auto-decided in either direction.

evidence.claim: NSE has revoked the debarment order against this subject.

explanation: Risk DE-ESCALATED from HIGH to MONITOR. The underlying NSE debarment has been revoked [EV-030]. The system reduces risk when the evidence base weakens, not only when it strengthens.

evidence.claim: Customer name matches a bare-token alias on the UAPA list.
evidence.excerpt: 'Amir Khan' is one of 97 single-token aliases; it is also an extremely common Indian name.

explanation: No alert raised. The only match was to the bare alias 'Amir Khan' [EV-040], which carries alias_quality=bare_token. Bare tokens require corroboration and may never trigger an alert on their own.

evidence.claim: Subject is a sitting member of Parliament (Independent).

explanation: EDD tier. The subject is a sitting MP [EV-050]. PEP status triggers enhanced due diligence; it is NOT an adverse-media finding and must not be presented as one.
